data.content: I can see the project — the express delivery test expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00`, so that'll fail. Let me start `dotnet watch test` for you:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` to get hot reload for their tests. Let me start it up for them. I can see that the express test will fail (expects 15.00 but the method returns 10.00).

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running and waiting for file changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is live — whenever you save a change to `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. You can now iterate on fixing the `CalculateShipping` method (the `"express"` case returns `10.00m` instead of `15.00m`).
